description:
What is the Total Loss of a Car?
When the estimated cost of repairing a damaged car is more than 75 per cent of its Insured Declared Value (IDV), then it is known as a total loss of the car. A car is declared as a total loss by the motor insurance company after a claim is raised by the policyholder for severe damages or theft. Moreover, if the estimated cost of repairing or replacing the insured car exceeds its actual market value, it is known as a constructive total loss. When a car is declared as a total loss by the motor insurance provider, it is called a totaled car.

The total loss of a car can occur in two situations, which are as follows:

Theft: When your car has been stolen and can't be recovered.
Severe Damages: When your car gets damaged to such a degree that you will have to almost spend the cost of the car to get it repaired.
In both situations, it makes no sense to repair the car due to its high repair cost. Hence, damaged cars are declared as a total loss in these situations.

How Do Insurance Companies Determine the Total Loss Value of a Car?

The total loss value of a car is estimated by a motor insurance company after you raise a claim. Once the claim has been made, your motor insurance company will send an adjuster to estimate the damages sustained by your car. The adjuster will carefully analyse various parts of the damaged car and roughly estimate the cost of repairing the car. If the repairing cost of the damaged car exceeds its current market value, the adjuster will declare it as a total loss.
total loss in car insurance is declared when a vehicle is damaged to such an extent that the cost of repair is higher than the vehicle's total IDV. Generally, the total loss is declared when the repair cost of the damaged vehicle exceeds 75% of the vehicle's IDV.
What is the Total Loss Car Insurance Claim Process?
The process to raise a total loss car insurance claim is given below:

Inform your motor insurer as soon as your car gets damaged
Provide all the requested information to the adjuster sent by the insurance company
The adjuster will assess the damages to the car and will either approve it for repair or declare it as a total loss.
Once the insured car has been declared a total loss, the adjuster will determine the actual cash value of the totalled car.
The insurer will pay the actual cash value of the totalled car.
#totalgaminglive loss claim,#car insurance claim,#total loss,#insurance claim,#total loss settlement,#total loss car insurance settlement,#how to find statement of claim,#claim settlement ratio,#claim settlement procedure,#total loss car,#car insurance claim settlement full information 2021,#accident claim in india,#car insurance claim process,#क्या आपका वाहन भी चोरी हो गया है ?,#total loss car value calculator,#total loss car accident,#no claim bonus car insurance malayalam

comments.text:
PSU insurers offer car insurance in least premium and have best claim settlement. They usually charge almost 40-50% less than getting insurance from dealers. 
Claim settlement ratio is a vague concept to measure efficiency of an insurance company. You should instead focus on amount of claim settlement by companies. Pvt cos are known for settling small claim very swiftly and are notorious for denying (on any flimsy grounds) or under-settling big claims. So they can easily maintain good claim settlement ratio despite having far lesser amount settlement ratio.
PSUs have backing of govt and claim settlement is transparent and generous irrespective of the claim amount. They simple reason is: they have to strictly follow IRDAI guidelines and PSUs have social service as priority not brute profits. And now a days they match or even exceed the TAT( turn around time) compared to PVT companies. If you check the no of complaints against insurers in website of Ombudsman, Beema lokpal or IRDAI, private companies top the disgraceful list and govt companies are least tarnished.
Now,the question arises why dealers or online portals / web aggregators don't prefer PSUs
1) Huge commission paid by Pvt cos to dealers and online web aggregators. So, they sideline PSU insurers and charge hefty premiums from unsuspecting customers so that they can enjoy mammoth share of your premium as commission, totally flouting IRDAI rules.
2) The dealers and web aggregators have to scrupulously comply with IRDAI guidelines while dealing with PSU insurers which will reduce any foul play or   unfair practices which are meant for safeguarding customers against any fraud. Remember,if your claim is stuck by dealer or Pvt cos,you are nowhere to go but in govt companies, strict compliance saves any injustice. So dealers want monopoly in their hands not yours.
Thus, you can draw the inference as to why PSU insurers should the only choice while buying insurance for your loved vehicle.
The 4 renowned PSU/ Govt insurance companies are
1) UNITED INDIA INSURANCE CO. LTD
2) NEW INDIA ASSURANCE CO. LTD.
3) NATIONAL INSURANCE CO. LTD.
4) ORIENTAL INSURANCE CO. LTD.
So, next time you buy a new vehicle,never purchase insurance from dealer instead approach PSU office and get cover for your vehicle. And whenever you renew your cover, do the same .
Drive safe.
comments.author: @funvideoswithvivek5900
